meta.source: The Tally Room — Victoria 2022 election dataset (Ben Raue), based on VEC declared results
meta.flowMatrixSource: VEC Distribution-of-Preferences pages (per-row URLs) where measured; proxy or conventional estimate where flagged measured: false
meta.notes:
Registered Group Voting Tickets (LC) imported from the VEC's 2022
registered-tickets archive (via Antony Green's reformatted ABC PDFs).
Each group's `ticket` array is the group-level order of TICKET 1 only —
Victorian groups could register up to 3 tickets and ATL votes split
1/3·1/3·1/3 across them, which this model approximates as "follow
ticket 1" (see assumption `lc-gvt-ticket-1-only`).

grn.source: Composite of (a) Vic 2022 measured Samiotis/Albert Park R6 exclusion (alp 84% / lib 16%, 8,488/1,639 of 10,127) and (b) SA 2026 ALP-vs-ONP finals (10-seat sample: alp 81% / onp 19%, per Antony Green rolling summary). Row calibrated so leftover-redistribution under `lib/seats/irv.ts:75-87` produces ~85/15 in ALP-vs-Lib 2CPs and ~80/20 in ALP-vs-ONP 2CPs.
grn.assumption: Grn → ALP 75% / Lib 5% / ONP 15% / Oth 5%. Reserves an explicit ONP destination at 15% so seats where ONP is in the final 2CP don't rely on leftover-redistribution alone (which under-credited ONP). In ALP-vs-Lib finals where ONP is absent, the 15% reservation re-spreads via leftover across survivors and reproduces the 85/15 split.

oth.source: Conventional psephology estimate, not measured for 2022 in this revision (would require aggregating exclusion rounds for ONP/UAP/FFV/LDP/AJP/Reason/LegalCannabis across many seats).
oth.assumption: Other/minor → ALP 44% / Lib 40% / Nat 8% / Grn 8% (proportional renormalisation of the earlier 42/38/8/8/4 split that included a meaningless 4% Oth→Oth self-loop; self-loops are filtered out of IRV redistribution in lib/seats/irv.ts and rendered as the diagonal in the UI matrix, so they don't contribute to the row sum).

nat.source: Proxied from SA2026 Lib→ONP measurement (no direct Nat→ONP data: only 3 Nat candidates ran in SA 2026, all polling too poorly for their exclusions to yield a clean flow estimate). Assumed slightly higher than Lib's 68% on the basis that Nat voters are rural-skewed and at least as ONP-friendly as Liberals; plausible band 65–80%, midpoint chosen.
nat.assumption: Nat → ONP 70% / Lib 22% / ALP 6% / Grn 1% / Oth 1% (post-Hopper realignment). Per-seat preferenceOverrides on the 6 Vic 2022 country 3-corner seats (bass, euroa, mildura, morwell, narracan, shepparton) preserve the prior Nat → Lib 82% Coalition-partner flow in seats where Lib actually stands as Nat's Coalition opponent.

lib.source: SA2026 aggregate: Liberal preferences flowed to One Nation at ~68% across 7 ALP-vs-ONP final contests (range 53% Enfield → 73.5% Hammond). Liberal HTV cards directed ONP > ALP in every SA 2026 seat — a post-Hopper realignment from the 2022 status quo. Sources: Antony Green rolling SA2026 summary (https://antonygreen.com.au/sa2026-updating-result-summary/); Ben Raue / Tally Room (https://www.tallyroom.com.au/64676); Murray Bridge News (Hammond 73.5%).
lib.assumption: Lib → ONP 68% / ALP 30% / Grn 1% / Oth 1% (SA2026-style realignment). Replaces the 2022 measured Morwell+Euroa composite (Lib → Nat 88% / ALP 12%), which was specific to country 3-corner Lib-vs-Nat contests; that flow is preserved via per-seat preferenceOverrides on bass, euroa, mildura, morwell, narracan, shepparton where the Coalition-partner dynamic still dominates.

alp.source: Conventional psephology estimate. ALP was rarely excluded in the DoPs sampled (mostly the major-party making 2CP), and when excluded (e.g. Schween/Mornington round 3 with only 822 votes) the round had too many remaining candidates to give a clean ALP→{Grn,Lib} split.
alp.assumption: ALP (if excluded) → Grn 55% / Lib 20% / Nat 8% / Oth 17%

onp.source: Composite of (a) AEC 2025 federal TPP preference flow table (ONP → L/NP 74.50% / ALP 25.50%, 252,917 ONP TPP votes in classic ALP-vs-LNP seats — https://results.aec.gov.au/31496/Website/HouseStateTppFlow-31496-NAT.htm); (b) Antony Green's SA 2026 rolling result summary (6 Lab-vs-Lib finals where ONP was last excluded: Lib 69% / ALP 31% — https://antonygreen.com.au/sa2026-updating-result-summary/); (c) 2022 VEC LA DoPs where ONP was excluded in Bendigo East, Macedon, Morwell (small N; ONP polled only 0.22% LA statewide in 2022). The 74.5% federal Coalition share is split into Lib and Nat on the assumption most VIC ONP-strong seats are metro/regional non-3-corner contests where only Lib runs; Nat share kept small and concentrated onto country 3-corner seats via per-seat preferenceOverrides.
onp.assumption: One Nation → Lib 67% / Nat 2% / ALP 27% / Grn 2% / Teal 1% / Oth 1% (Coalition aggregate 69%, matching SA 2026 6-seat ALP-vs-Lib final measurement of 69/31). Nudged from prior 70/22 split: ALP up to 27% (closer to SA's 31% but kept below it because the SA sample is biased to seats where ALP primary was unusually low), Coalition share down a touch. Nat reservation shrunk to 2% (most ONP-strong Vic seats are metro/regional non-3-corner contests; Nat share will be re-routed by leftover redistribution in the few country seats). Teal 1% reflects that a handful of ONP voters put community indies above Labor/Greens; auto-normalised away by IRV in seats without a teal candidate.

ind_teal.source: https://www.vec.vic.gov.au/results/state-election-results/2022-state-election-results/results-by-district/hawthorn-district-results/hawthorn-results-distribution + .../kew-district-results/kew-results-distribution + corroborating: .../mornington-district-results/mornington-results-distribution (Grn→Lardner 83.8% in round 6, consistent with Lardner being a progressive-leaning indie)
ind_teal.assumption: Teal → ALP 75% / Lib 25% (measured composite from Hawthorn + Kew: Lowe 9,219/2,839 of 12,058; Torney 8,379/3,092 of 11,471 — weighted 17,598/5,931 of 23,529 → ALP 74.8% / Lib 25.2%). Greens already excluded in both measured contests; small Grn share reserved on the assumption some teal voters had Grn above Lib. Lardner/Mornington made 2CP so her own flows are unobservable — proxied via Hawthorn+Kew; the Grn→Lardner 84% flow in Mornington corroborates her teal profile.

ind_country.source: Proxied from Altmann (South-West Coast) measured flow + community-indie convention. Direct measurement impossible: Hawkins (Benambra), Cupper (Mildura) and Sheed (Shepparton) all made 2CP in their seats, so their preferences were never distributed.
ind_country.assumption: Country indie → ALP 55% / Lib 22% / Nat 15% / Grn 5% / Oth 3% (Altmann-shaped, with Nat share added since these contests are 3-cornered Coalition).

nat.assumption: Bass had no Nationals candidate; YAML's `nat: 5,506` corresponds to Bugbird (Shooters, Fishers & Farmers). Real DoP: Bugbird R7 exclusion sent 3,301/1,122/626 of 5,049 → grn 65.4% / lib 22.2% / alp 12.4%. In this model Brown (Grn) is excluded R1 (lowest primary) so the SFF→Greens preference cannot stay with Greens; the override encodes the EFFECTIVE rollup flow when grn is unavailable (SFF voters fall back to Lib > oth > ALP). Calibrated so IRV reproduces the recorded 50.24% 2CP exactly.

oth.assumption: Aggregate of Edwards/Mousellis/Munday/Jobe/O'Neill/Verhagen oth-bucket exclusions; in the final stage where only alp & lib remain, the net flow is slightly more pro-Lib than statewide (Verhagen/Family First → Lib 46.3% being the largest single contributor). Calibrated alongside the nat row to reproduce alp 50.24% 2CP.

lib.assumption: 3-cornered country contest with strong Coalition-partner flow. Measured Hearn (Lib) R-last exclusion: 9,046/1,353 of 10,399 → nat 87.0% / alp 13.0%. Override preserves the Coalition-partner Lib→Nat flow in this seat; statewide row routes Lib→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests.

nat.source: Proxied; Nat won Euroa (winnerPct 59.93) so preferences not distributed in the actual DoP. Override preserves Coalition-partner flow under scenarios where Nat could be eliminated.
nat.assumption: Country Nat → Lib 82% / ALP 12% / Grn 2% / Oth 4% (prior statewide Vic 2022 estimate). Statewide row now routes Nat→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests; this override keeps the Coalition-partner flow in seats where Lib is the Nat's actual Coalition counterpart.

grn.assumption: Grn → ind_teal 53.7% / alp 37.0% / lib 9.3% (measured: Savage, Grn, excluded round 5; 2837/1956/491 of 5284). Statewide grn row over-routes to ALP in seats where a teal stands.

alp.source: Proxied from Mornington ALP→{teal,lib} measured flow (Fowler/Mornington round 6; 8927/1722 of 10649 → ind_teal 83.8% / lib 16.2%). Direct measurement impossible in Hawthorn — ALP/Kennedy made 2CP.

grn.assumption: Grn → ind_teal 47.9% / alp 40.7% / lib 11.3% (measured: Carter, Grn, excluded round 5; 1881/1598/445 of 3924). Statewide grn row over-routes to ALP in seats where a teal stands.

alp.source: Proxied from Mornington ALP→{teal,lib} measured flow (Fowler/Mornington round 6; 8927/1722 of 10649 → ind_teal 83.8% / lib 16.2%). Direct measurement impossible in Kew — ALP/Skelton made 2CP.

lib.assumption: Inner-city Lib voters preferenced Greens above ALP. Measured Palackalody R7 exclusion: 5,851/2,724 of 8,575 → grn 68.2% / alp 31.8%. Statewide lib row's 0.85 to nat is irrelevant here (no Nat candidate) and the leftover-redistribute under-credits grn.

oth.assumption: Aggregate Vic Socialists + Reason + minor-left oth-bucket exclusions flow strongly to Greens (e.g. Bolger/Vic Socialists round 6: 2,524 of 3,172 to Sandell).

lib.source: Proxied from Morwell+Euroa measured Vic 2022 country 3-corner composite (Lib→Nat 87.9%). Mildura's actual 2CP was Nat vs ind_country (Cupper), so Lib's preferences flowed primarily to Nat per Coalition ticket.
lib.assumption: Country 3-corner Lib→Nat per Coalition ticket. Override preserves the Coalition-partner Lib→Nat flow; statewide row routes Lib→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests.

nat.source: Proxied; Nat won Mildura (winnerPct 51.18) so preferences not distributed in the actual DoP. Override preserves Coalition-partner flow under scenarios where Nat could be eliminated.
nat.assumption: Country Nat → Lib 82% / ALP 12% / Grn 2% / Oth 4% (prior statewide Vic 2022 estimate).

grn.assumption: Grn → ind_teal 50.5% / alp 38.3% / lib 11.2% (measured: Sinclair, Grn, excluded round 5; 1589/1204/352 of 3145). Statewide grn row over-routes to ALP in seats where a teal stands.

alp.assumption: ALP → ind_teal 83.8% / lib 16.2% (measured: Fowler, ALP, excluded round 6; 8927/1722 of 10649). This row is the source of the 83.8% figure proxied to Hawthorn and Kew.

lib.assumption: 3-cornered country contest with strong Coalition-partner flow. Measured Harriman R-last exclusion: 9,658/1,225 of 10,883 → nat 88.7% / alp 11.3%. Override preserves the Coalition-partner Lib→Nat flow; statewide row routes Lib→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests.

nat.source: Proxied; Nat won Morwell (winnerPct 54.42) so preferences not distributed in the actual DoP. Override preserves Coalition-partner flow under scenarios where Nat could be eliminated.
nat.assumption: Country Nat → Lib 82% / ALP 12% / Grn 2% / Oth 4% (prior statewide Vic 2022 estimate). Statewide row now routes Nat→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests; this override keeps the Coalition-partner flow in seats where Lib is Nat's actual Coalition counterpart.

oth.assumption: Aggregate SFF/IMO/Family First/independent oth-bucket flows toward Nat in 3-cornered country contest at substantially higher rate than statewide 0.08.

seats.name: Narracan
seats.calibration: notional 2022 result — see assumption `narracan-notional` for methodology (primaries = mean share-of-formal from Morwell + Bass; 2CP anchored to 2018 pendulum Lib +5.7 and 2023 supplementary)

lib.source: Notional seat (Gilchrist death voided 2022 ballot). Lib actually wins Narracan in this notional scenario (2CP lib 55.7%), so Lib preferences would not be distributed; override exists for safety under swung scenarios where Lib could be eliminated.
lib.assumption: Country 3-corner Lib→Nat per Coalition ticket. Override preserves the Coalition-partner Lib→Nat flow; statewide row routes Lib→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests.

nat.source: Synthesised; Coalition-style lib-dominant Nat flow consistent with comparable 3-cornered easternVictoria seats. No real DoP exists — Gilchrist died mid-campaign.
nat.assumption: Notional Nat preferences flow to Lib (Coalition ticket).

oth.source: Synthesised to reproduce the notional 2CP (lib 55.7%); shape proxied from Morwell/Bass oth-bucket behaviour. No real DoP exists.
oth.assumption: Notional oth flow tuned to reproduce notional 2CP of lib 55.7% / alp 44.3%.

lib.assumption: Moon's R6 accumulated exclusion of 8,370 sent 5,702 to ALP and 2,668 to Greens (68.1% / 31.9%), but that pool included 914 votes of earlier minor-party reflow. After stripping the reflow contribution, the pure Lib-primary split is closer to even — calibrated to alp 0.475 / grn 0.525 so IRV reproduces the recorded 57.32% 2CP exactly.

oth.assumption: Vic Socialists + AJP + Reason + others flow overwhelmingly to Greens in inner Richmond (e.g. Ward/Vic Socialists R5: 2,194 of 2,858 = 76.8% to De Vietri).

lib.source: Proxied from Morwell+Euroa measured Vic 2022 country 3-corner composite (Lib→Nat 87.9%). Shepparton's actual 2CP was Nat vs ind_country (Sheed), so Lib's preferences flowed primarily to Nat per Coalition ticket.
lib.assumption: Country 3-corner Lib→Nat per Coalition ticket. Override preserves the Coalition-partner Lib→Nat flow; statewide row routes Lib→ONP for ALP-vs-ONP-style contests.

nat.source: Proxied; Nat won Shepparton (winnerPct 56.79) so preferences not distributed in the actual DoP. Override preserves Coalition-partner flow under scenarios where Nat could be eliminated.
nat.assumption: Country Nat → Lib 82% / ALP 12% / Grn 2% / Oth 4% (prior statewide Vic 2022 estimate).

assumptions.summary: Primary and 2CP vote counts sourced from The Tally Room (Ben Raue) 2022 Victoria dataset, which republishes VEC declared results.
assumptions.rationale: Tally Room is the most credible tidied republication of Australian electoral data; uses VEC declared figures directly.
assumptions.impact: Numbers should match VEC declared 2022 results to the vote.

assumptions.summary: Statewide preference flow rates are MEASURED from VEC distribution-of-preferences pages where the source candidate was excluded and we could observe the split: Greens (Albert Park), Liberal (Morwell + Euroa), and the indie row for Hawthorn/Kew (ind_teal). Each row carries the source URL and a `measured: true` flag. Per-seat `preferenceOverrides` carry additional measured rows local to a single seat — see `teal-flows-seat-specific`.
assumptions.rationale: Direct measurement is more credible than statewide-average estimates for the contests that drive the model.
assumptions.impact: Measured rows are a snapshot of one (or two) DoP contest(s) — not a true statewide weighted average. A seat with unusual local dynamics may still diverge unless covered by a per-seat override.

assumptions.summary: For sources never excluded in the contests sampled (ALP, Nat, and minor-party `oth`), and for indie candidates who made 2CP (Hawkins/Benambra, Cupper/Mildura, Sheed/Shepparton, Lardner/Mornington), flows are either proxied (Lardner via Hawthorn+Kew; ind_country via Altmann's South-West Coast measurement) or estimated. Those rows carry `measured: false` (or note proxy in `source:`).
assumptions.rationale: Honest provenance — these rows should be reviewed and improved by additional DoP sampling rather than left silently estimated.
assumptions.impact: Estimate accuracy is conventional-psephology level (within ~5pp of typical Vic state averages) but not pegged to 2022 specifically.

assumptions.summary: Two indie groupings broken out: ind_teal (Hawthorn/Kew/Mornington — statewide ind_teal row measured from Hawthorn+Kew teal exclusions; per-seat `preferenceOverrides` carry the additional Grn and ALP flows measured in each seat) and ind_country (Benambra/Mildura/Shepparton — all made 2CP, proxied via Altmann's South-West Coast measurement). All other independents — including the >10%-primary one-off indies (Greco/Preston, Seymour/Rowville, Altmann/South-West Coast, Cook/Mulgrave) and sub-10% indies — are folded into the `oth` bucket.
assumptions.rationale: Bundling tests against observable DoP data: Hawthorn + Kew measured ind_teal flows are close enough (74.8/25.2 weighted) to share the statewide row. Lardner's own out-flow is unmeasurable (she made 2CP) but Mornington's Greens and ALP exclusions did happen, and both flow heavily to her — consistent with her teal profile. The one-off indies in 2022 don't form a coherent statewide phenomenon, so are treated like any other minor.
assumptions.impact: One-off indie seats (Preston, Rowville, South-West Coast, Mulgrave) lose their measured flow signal — their preferences now flow via the conventional `oth` estimate. Seats where a <10% indie matters strategically remain under-modelled.

assumptions.summary: Hawthorn, Kew, and Mornington carry per-seat `preferenceOverrides` for the `grn` and `alp` rows. Measured Greens flows: Hawthorn (Savage round 5, ind_teal 53.7% / alp 37.0% / lib 9.3%), Kew (Carter round 5, 47.9 / 40.7 / 11.3), Mornington (Sinclair round 5, 50.5 / 38.3 / 11.2). Measured ALP flow (Mornington only): Fowler round 6, ind_teal 83.8% / lib 16.2%; that figure is proxied to Hawthorn and Kew via `measured: false` overrides because in those seats ALP made 2CP and its flow is unobservable.
assumptions.rationale: Without seat-level overrides, the statewide `grn` row (measured in Albert Park where no teal stood) routes 82% of Greens prefs to ALP in every seat — including Hawthorn/Kew/Mornington, where in reality the teal indie collected the largest share. Similarly the statewide `alp` row contains no `ind_teal` destination at all, so when ALP is excluded in a Lib-vs-teal contest its preferences split ~60/40 in the leftover-mass redistribution rather than the ~84/16 we actually measured in Mornington. Both effects systematically under-counted the teal vote.
assumptions.impact: Hawthorn, Kew, and Mornington now respond correctly to swings on the ind_teal slider — a small swing flips Mornington (real 2022 margin: 50.7% Lib), a larger swing flips Kew or Hawthorn. The other 85 seats are unchanged.

assumptions.summary: Lib + Nat combined for LA 2CP reporting, separate for primaries and LC.
assumptions.rationale: Matches VEC convention; Coalition partners exchange preferences strongly via tickets.

assumptions.summary: Default uniform per-party swing; proportional (pendulum-aware) offered as a toggle.
assumptions.rationale: Uniform swing is the standard sensitivity tool; proportional better reflects real swings but is opinionated.
assumptions.impact: Toggling between modes will move marginal seats.

assumptions.summary: LC uses Weighted Inclusive Gregory, matching VEC 2022 counting rules.
assumptions.rationale: Each contributing bundle is re-weighted by the transfer value (TV = surplus / current pile value) on every surplus distribution; prior TVs compound multiplicatively. This is the formula in the Electoral Act 2002 (Vic) and what VEC implemented for the 2022 LC count.
assumptions.impact: Surplus transfers track the official method; remaining seat-allocation differences from the VEC declared result come from BTL preference behaviour we can't observe without ballot-paper data, not from the transfer arithmetic.

assumptions.summary: LC model uses Group Voting Tickets (still in force in Victoria as of 2026).
assumptions.rationale: Victoria is the last Australian jurisdiction to retain GVTs; the 2022 election used them and 2026 will too.
assumptions.impact: Above-the-line votes follow registered tickets deterministically.

assumptions.summary: Each group is modelled as a single voting block, not as N individual candidates.
assumptions.rationale: Real STV elects one candidate at a time; surplus from an elected candidate flows down the ticket, which in Victoria's GVTs lists the elected group's OWN remaining candidates first (positions 2-5) before reaching other groups. The model collapses each group's per-candidate tallies into one group tally, so when a group hits quota it can only be 'elected' once before its surplus moves to the next group — bypassing the within-group flow that in reality elects a major party's 2nd candidate.
assumptions.impact: Surplus from an elected group flows down its ticket to the next group, never to a running-mate inside the same group. Against the declared 2022 LC result this now produces a 2-seat error: ALP (15), Coalition combined (14), Greens (4), Legalise Cannabis (2), One Nation, Animal Justice and SFF all reproduce exactly. The model differs in two regions — Western Metro, where it elects DLP for the 5th seat (actual: Trung Luu / Liberal), and South-Eastern Metro, where it elects a 2nd Liberal (actual: David Limbrick / Libertarians). Net party-tally error: DLP +1, LDP −1; Coalition combined unchanged. Closing the gap requires either (a) tracking per-candidate within each group or (b) allowing a group to be re-elected while its remaining tally ≥ quota.

assumptions.summary: LA exhaust rate set to 0 (compulsory full preferences).
assumptions.rationale: Victoria LA requires numbering every box; informal votes are excluded from `formal` count.
assumptions.impact: All preferences flow; no exhaust pile in LA cascade.

assumptions.summary: Below-the-line LC votes folded into their first preference's group; BTL not modelled separately.
assumptions.rationale: BTL is ~10% of LC votes; cleanly modelling it would require ballot-paper-level data.
assumptions.impact: Edge-case groups whose elections turn on BTL flows are missed.

assumptions.summary: Narracan's 2022 ballot was voided after Nationals candidate Shaun Gilchrist died mid-campaign; the supplementary election (2023-01-28, won by Wayne Farnham Lib 63.0% 2CP vs Wolfe Ind) is unusable as a 2022 stand-in because ALP and the Nationals declined to contest it. To avoid Narracan sticking out in tallies and scenarios, the entry is a synthesised notional 2022 result.
assumptions.rationale:
Methodology:
1. Comparable seats: Morwell and Bass — both easternVictoria, both
   genuine three-cornered Lib/Nat/ALP races, both demographically
   close to Narracan's West Gippsland geography (Latrobe Valley
   fringe + commuter-rural). Gippsland East/South were excluded as
   comparables: they are rural Nat strongholds where the Liberals
   do not contest, which would over-weight Nat share for Narracan.
2. Primaries: mean of Morwell's and Bass's share-of-formal-vote per
   party, applied to a projected formal of 41,500 (midpoint of the
   two comparables' formal counts: Morwell 43,261; Bass 41,404).
   Per-party shares used: alp 32.0%, lib 22.8%, nat 18.1%, grn 6.2%,
   oth 20.9%. Resulting counts (rounded, total 41,500):
   alp 13,280; lib 9,462; nat 7,512; grn 2,573; oth 8,673.
3. 2CP: Lib vs ALP at 55.7%. Anchored to Narracan's 2018 pendulum
   (Blackwood, Lib +5.7 2PP), tempered toward an estimated 2022 ALP
   swing of ~3pp consistent with neighbouring outcomes, and
   cross-checked against the 2023 supplementary in which Farnham
   (Lib) polled 45% on primaries in a soft field — strong evidence
   the seat remained safely Lib in 2022.
Sensitivity: a wider Lib/Nat split or a tighter 2CP (e.g. Lib 52)
would still leave the seat in the Liberal column under zero swing.
assumptions.impact: Narracan now contributes 1 Lib seat to the LA tally on the same basis as every other seat (LA total 88, ALP 56 / Lib 19 / Nat 9 / Grn 4). Previously the placeholder 2CP already gave Lib the win, so the total is unchanged.

assumptions.summary: Every LA seat reproduces its 2022 winner under zero swing (88/88).
assumptions.rationale: Baseline correctness is the model's credibility floor.
assumptions.impact: Five seats required per-seat `preferenceOverrides` beyond the statewide flow matrix to match the recorded twoCp winner: bass (knife-edge 50.24%; YAML's `nat` corresponds to Bugbird/SFF which can't follow statewide nat-row flows), melbourne and richmond (inner-city seats where Lib HTV and progressive-minor flows differ from statewide), morwell (3-cornered country contest with strong Coalition flow to Nat), and narracan (synthesised notional seat — see `narracan-notional`). Margins now match recorded twoCp.winnerPct within ±2pp for all five.

assumptions.summary: 23 seats had their preference distribution halted before reaching the final two candidates, so seat-level 2CP is a sum of notional booth-level counts. Hawthorn, Kew and Shepparton have 2CP pairings that differ between booth count and final distribution.
assumptions.rationale: Per Tally Room's VIC-2022-Notes.txt. Affects edge-case seats where the 2CP loser is non-obvious.
assumptions.impact: The 2CP winnerPct figures in those seats reflect Tally Room's blend, not a clean VEC final-distribution number.

assumptions.summary: Tally Room snapshot taken from their Google Drive folder as of 2026-05-17. Files dated 2023-01 to 2025-08. DoP citations fetched live from vec.vic.gov.au on 2026-05-17.
assumptions.rationale: Locking the snapshot makes the model reproducible.
